_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: The Highway Contracts You Are Not Allowed to Read
context: Works Ministry confirmed that 21 toll concession agreements remain classified under the Official Secrets Act despite repeated parliamentary questions since 2008.

cards.text: You pay the toll. You cannot read the contract.
cards.sub: Twenty-one concession agreements. All classified. Exposed clauses show guaranteed traffic volumes.

cards.h: Leaked clauses reveal government compensation if traffic falls below targets.
cards.s: Taxpayers pay toll companies when people drive less. The incentive is perverse.
cards.d: Leaked clauses reveal government compensation if traffic falls below targets. Taxpayers pay toll companies when people drive less. The incentive is perverse.

cards.h: Toll revenue: RM5.1B in 2025. Government compensation: RM1.2B on top.
cards.s: The public pays twice: at the booth and through taxes.
cards.d: Toll revenue: RM5.1B in 2025. Government compensation: RM1.2B on top. The public pays twice: at the booth and through taxes.

cards.h: PH promised declassification in 2018. Retracted within 6 months.
cards.s: Every government that enters office discovers why secrecy is convenient.
cards.d: PH promised declassification in 2018. Retracted within 6 months. Every government that enters office discovers why secrecy is convenient.

cards.text: What kind of contract requires secrecy from the people paying for it?
cards.sub: Classification protects terms, not security. Ask whose interests that serves.

cards.text: Like paying for a gym membership where the terms are classified. You pay every month but cannot see what you signed up for.
cards.sub: Taxpayers fund toll roads but are legally barred from reading the contracts that determine the cost

cards.text: A contract between government and a private company, paid by the public, classified from the public. The structure answers its own question.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
